title: Wireshark Theme
description: Color theme definition for Wireshark and Stratoshark. The actual file format is JSONC (JSON with C-style comments); this schema validates the post-comment-stripped JSON. Sections sit at the top level of the document — `brand`, `accent`, `expert`, etc. — matching what `ThemeParser` consumes.

meta.description: Theme metadata displayed in the preferences dialog and used for identification.

name.description: Display name of the theme, e.g. "Wireshark Default".

version.description: Schema version. Must be 1 for the current format.

description.description: One-line description shown in the preferences dialog.

brand.description: Two colors that define the theme's visual identity. `primary` drives header gradient end, selection highlight, section header hover, link accents. `deep` drives header gradient start.

accent.description: Four semantic accent colors. Drive the update bar, filter validation, status indicators, expert info fallbacks, and the Development Build badge.

expert.description: Optional expert-info severity colors. When omitted, derived from accent values at 40% alpha.

packets.description: Optional packet-display state colors. For each state the unsuffixed key is the row background tint; the matching *Text key is the foreground. `hidden` is text-only (no background) — it dims items rendered on the normal row bg. When a *Text key is omitted, the foreground is derived via contrastingText() against the bg. When `ignored` (bg) is omitted, the row's normal background is used unchanged.

conversation.description: Optional Follow Stream conversation colors. `client`/`server` are background tints, `clientText`/`serverText` are foregrounds. When the bg keys are omitted: client = accent.error tint on base; server = brand.primary tint on base. When the *Text keys are omitted: clientText is derived from accent.error (darken in light mode, lighten in dark); serverText is derived from brand.primary the same way.

palette.description: Optional QPalette role overrides. Selective merge: only specified roles replace the system default. `Highlight`, `Link`, and (Qt 6.6+) `Accent` are always overridden from brand.primary regardless of this section. `HighlightedText` is auto-derived via contrastingText(brand.primary) but can be pinned here for themes whose brand colour sits on the WCAG luminance threshold.

filter.description: Optional filter-validity tint overrides. Every key is independently optional; omitted ones derive from accent + palette.base. `valid`, `invalid`, `deprecated` are bg tints for the filter-edit field. `busy` and `busyText` are the bg + fg used while a capture filter is being compiled off-thread (the legacy GTK behavior, exposed here so themes can pin a more distinct visual than the default placeholder fade). Explicit values let themes ship the historical Tango saturated-dark-green/dark-red look that alpha-tinting an accent can never reach.

separator.description: Optional hairline/divider color used between cards and sections. When omitted, derived as a 53% mix of palette.base toward palette.mid. Themes whose palette.mid doubles as muted text (e.g. Stratoshark) should set this explicitly to a softer value so dividers stay subtle.

graphs.description: Optional rotating color cycle for I/O graphs, sequence/flow diagrams, TCP stream plots, RTP player, and IAX2 analysis. Custom themes may provide any number of colors; no backfill from the default theme.

fonts.description: Optional font hints. User's `gui.fonts.qt.font_name` preference overrides the theme's monospace setting when set.

images.description: Reserved for future use; not currently consumed by ThemeParser.

syntax.description: Reserved for future use; not currently consumed by ThemeParser.

colorValue.description: A CSS color string: "#rrggbb", "#rrggbbaa", or "rgba(r,g,b,a)" with alpha 0-255 (QSS convention, NOT 0-1).

colorPair.description: A color token with light and dark variants. The parser tolerates a missing variant (warns and reuses the other), but valid themes specify both.

fontHint.description: Optional font specification. Both fields are optional; when omitted, platform defaults apply.

family.description: Font family name. When omitted, the system UI font (regular) or platform fixed-pitch chain (monospace) is used.

size.description: Point size. When omitted (or <= 0), platform default applies.
